title: hypericum obtusifolium
intro: Hypericum obtusifolium, also known as St. Andrew's cross or shrubby St. Andrew's cross, is a perennial flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family. It is a shrubby plant with small yellow flowers that have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including wound healing, pain relief, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Hypericum obtusifolium can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and reduce stress.
